name: Industrial IoT gateway
description: A device that bridges OT/IT networks, collects sensor data, and relays it to cloud or on-premise systems.

component_templates.name: Cloud connectivity agent
elicitation_questions: Does the device connect to a cloud platform or remote server?
Which protocol does it use? (e.g. MQTT, AMQP, HTTPS, proprietary)
Is the connection authenticated? If so, how? (certificates, API key, username/password)
Is the connection encrypted? Which TLS version?

component_templates.name: Firmware update agent
elicitation_questions: Does the device support firmware updates?
How are updates delivered? (OTA, local USB, serial port, SD card)
Are firmware images signed and verified before installation?
Is there rollback protection if an update fails?

component_templates.name: Local configuration interface
elicitation_questions: Does the device have a local configuration interface? (web UI, CLI, mobile app)
Which protocol or access method does it use? (HTTP, HTTPS, SSH, UART)
Is authentication required to access it?
Are default credentials changeable by the user?

component_templates.name: Sensor / field device interface
elicitation_questions: Which field protocols does the device support? (Modbus RTU/TCP, OPC-UA, PROFINET, CAN, 4-20mA, custom)
Is the field communication authenticated or encrypted in any way?
Can a rogue field device be injected without detection?

component_templates.name: Persistent data store
elicitation_questions: Does the device store data locally? (logs, buffered telemetry, configuration)
Is stored data encrypted at rest?
What happens to stored data if the device is physically removed or tampered with?

component_templates.name: Debug / JTAG interface
elicitation_questions: Does the device have a JTAG, UART debug, or similar hardware debug interface?
Is it disabled or protected in production units?
Is secure boot enabled to prevent unauthorised firmware loading?

component_templates.name: Network management interface
elicitation_questions: Does the device expose a network management interface? (SNMP, NETCONF, REST API)
Is it accessible from both OT and IT network sides?
Is it authenticated and encrypted?
Does data flow from field devices through the gateway to the cloud without transformation, or is it normalised?
Is there any filtering or validation of field data before forwarding?
Are firmware update packages delivered over the same cloud channel as telemetry, or a separate channel?
Can the cloud platform initiate a firmware push without operator confirmation on the device?
Can a local admin read or export stored telemetry data through the configuration interface?
Are configuration changes logged to the persistent store?
Can the debug interface be used to extract credentials or keys stored in the device?
Is the bootloader protected against modification via debug interface?

threat_hints.title: Spoofed cloud endpoint
threat_hints.description: An attacker impersonates the cloud broker, intercepting telemetry and injecting commands.
threat_hints.mitigation: Mutual TLS with pinned server certificate

threat_hints.title: Unsigned firmware accepted
threat_hints.description: A malicious firmware image is installed if signature verification is absent or bypassable.
threat_hints.mitigation: Hardware-rooted signature verification; reject unsigned images

threat_hints.title: Rogue Modbus master
threat_hints.description: Modbus has no authentication; any device on the OT network can issue read/write commands.
threat_hints.mitigation: Network segmentation; allowlist of permitted Modbus masters by MAC/IP

threat_hints.title: Privilege escalation via debug interface
threat_hints.description: An attacker with physical access uses the JTAG interface to read memory, extract keys, or inject code.
threat_hints.mitigation: Disable JTAG in production fuses; enable secure boot

threat_hints.title: Tampered local logs
threat_hints.description: An attacker with local access modifies or deletes stored logs to cover tracks or corrupt audit trail.
threat_hints.mitigation: Append-only log storage; log integrity via HMAC

threat_hints.title: Default credentials on web UI
threat_hints.description: Factory-default admin credentials are not changed, allowing trivial takeover of local config interface.
threat_hints.mitigation: Force credential change on first boot; no universal default passwords
